title: 10 best herbal teas for strep throat
intro_desc: Herbal teas for strep throat are a natural and soothing way to alleviate symptoms of the infection, which can bring relief to those affected. These teas work by reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and promoting recovery. Examples of herbal teas that help with strep throat include slippery elm, peppermint, and ginger, which can ease sore throats and reduce fever. By drinking these teas, individuals can experience improved sleep, reduced discomfort, and a faster recovery process, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with strep throat because it contains compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the soreness and discomfort associated with this condition. The tea's flavonoids and glycosides also have antibacterial properties, which can aid in fighting off the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that causes strep throat. Additionally, licorice teas' soothing qualities can provide relief from a scratchy throat and cough, promoting a more comfortable recovery.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which can help combat the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause strep throat.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These bioactive compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and alleviate symptoms such as soreness and discomfort.
Liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin: These compounds have been shown to have antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, making them effective against strep throat infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sore throats, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by strep throat.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoids, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections like strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic glycyrrhiza glabra roots dried and crushed into fine powder.
Measure 1 teaspoon of the powdered root for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a teapot or infuser for 5 to 7 minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding solids and residue.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then consume immediately within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with strep throat because of its natural antibacterial properties, specifically targeting the bacteria that causes strep throat. The tea contains compounds like cinnamic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacterial strain responsible for most cases of strep throat. Additionally, coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of strep throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Kansas Coneflower tea contains antimicrobial compounds like echinacin, which help combat bacterial and viral infections, including streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria responsible for strep throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Kansas Coneflower tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with strep throat inflammation and infection.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower tea is believed to stimulate the immune system's natural response, enhancing the body's ability to fight off infections like strep throat by increasing white blood cell production and activity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, making it effective in fighting against bacterial infections like strep throat.
Barks: Containing tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried echinacea angustifolia root and pure mountain spring water in a medium-sized teapot.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried echinacea root for every eight ounces of water.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes to allow the herbs to release their beneficial properties slowly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles.
Drink the warm tea three times daily, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with strep throat because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to effectively combat Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause the infection. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce inflammation and soothe a sore throat, while also inhibiting bacterial growth and replication. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help boost the immune system, supporting the body's natural defense against infection and promoting quicker recovery from strep throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage tea has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections like strep throat by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of harmful bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage tea can reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, making it more comfortable to swallow and alleviating symptoms of strep throat such as soreness and redness.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tea is rich in antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic conditions like strep throat and reduce the severity of its symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antimicrobial compounds that help combat strep bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain salviarin, a compound that exhibits antibacterial properties, making them effective against strep throat infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle to a rolling boil for three to five minutes.
Steep the dried herbs in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids after one minute.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it within 15-30 minutes of preparation to help alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia teas helps with strep throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the swelling in the throat and alleviate pain. The antioxidants present in stevia also boost the body's immune system, enabling it to fight off the bacterial infection more effectively. Additionally, stevia's antibacterial compounds help to destroy the streptococcus bacteria that cause the infection, promoting a quicker recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal stevia teas contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat the bacterial infection caused by Streptococcus pyogenes, reducing the severity and duration of strep throat symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in herbal stevia teas help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and tonsils, making it easier to swallow and alleviating discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in herbal stevia teas neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting a faster recovery from strep throat infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stevia plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with strep throat.
Barks: The barks of the stevia plant contain tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as sore throats and swollen tonsils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stevia rebaudiana leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the stevia leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove impurities.
Combine one teaspoon of dried stevia leaves with one cup boiling water and steep.
Allow the tea to infuse for 10-15 minutes before straining and serving hot or cold.
Consume two cups of this herbal tea three times a day for optimal relief from strep throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with strep throat because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The menthol present in peppermint tea also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, which can aid in clearing out infected bacteria. Additionally, peppermint's warming properties help to ease congestion and reduce throat pain, providing relief from the scratchy and sore sensations associated with strep throat.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria, which causes strep throat, and soothe the affected areas.
Eugenol: Eugenol's anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate sore throats and reduce swelling in the throat and tonsils, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid's antioxidant properties can help combat oxidative stress caused by strep throat infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to soothe and calm sore throats.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh peppermint leaves or use dried peppermint flakes for brewing.
Combine the harvested mint leaves or dried peppermint flakes with one quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the menthol and antibacterial properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the prepared peppermint tea in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze it for later use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with strep throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease discomfort. The active compound gingerol has antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacteria responsible for strep throat, Streptococcus pyogenes. Additionally, ginger's expectorant nature can help loosen mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Overall, ginger teas provide a soothing and natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of strep throat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and neck, making it a natural remedy for relieving sore throats.

Antimicrobial properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in ginger tea have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria, including Streptococcus pyogenes, which is responsible for strep throat infections, helping to combat the infection and promote healing.

Expectorant properties: Ginger's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel, which can help alleviate congestion and coughing associated with strep throat.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation associated with strep throat.
Roots: Containing antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, the roots of the ginger plant are used to soothe sore throats and reduce pain caused by strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Gently boil one teaspoon of dried ginger root in two cups of water for five minutes.
Remove the tea from heat and let it steep for an additional seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea and help soothe the throat.
Drink the tea hot, three times a day, for up to five days as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with strep throat because its potent antimicrobial properties, specifically eucalyptol and cineole, combat the bacterial infection that causes strep. The tea's soothing and anti-inflammatory effects also ease symptoms such as sore throats and swollen tonsils. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to clear mucus and reduce congestion. As a natural remedy, Tasmanian blue gum tea provides a gentle and effective way to alleviate strep throat discomfort, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria that causes strep throat, which can help reduce the severity and duration of the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as sore throats, swelling, and pain associated with strep throat.
Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant help soothe the sore throat and reduce inflammation caused by strep throat.
Barks: Containing antibacterial properties, barks of the plant inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria that causes strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and measure out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Bring fresh, filtered water to a rolling boil in a tea kettle or pot and pour over the eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the aromatic oils and flavonoids into the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the leaves, retaining the soothing liquid.
Consume the eucalyptus globulus tea immediately after preparation while still warm, as the antimicrobial properties are most effective when consumed hot and fresh.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with strep throat because it contains berberine, a natural antibacterial compound that targets and eliminates the bacteria causing the infection. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing swelling and pain associated with strep throat. By drinking goldenseal tea regularly, individuals can help combat the infection and alleviate symptoms such as fever, soreness, and difficulty swallowing. The tea's antimicrobial properties also work to prevent the spread of the infection, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound found in Goldenseal, has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections like Strep Throat, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Berbamine: Berbamine, another alkaloid present in Goldenseal, exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to alleviate swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with Strep Throat.

Isoquinoline Alkaloids: The Isoquinoline alkaloids in Goldenseal tea possess immune-stimulating properties that help boost the body's natural defenses against infections, promoting faster recovery from Strep Throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a compound that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective against strep throat.
Rhizomes: Contain a higher concentration of berberine than roots, which helps to soothe and reduce inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hydrastis canadensis roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the roots in a warm oven at 150°f for two hours or air-dry for several days.
Combine one teaspoon of dried hydrastis canadensis root with one cup of boiling filtered water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Drink the tea three times a day, taking care to dilute with an equal amount of warm water if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with strep throat because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the swollen tonsils and reduce pain. The antioxidants in dandelion also help to combat infection by neutralizing free radicals, which can damage healthy cells and tissues. Additionally, dandelion's natural antimicrobial compounds target the streptococcus bacteria, helping to alleviate symptoms such as a sore throat, fever, and swollen lymph nodes. Drinking dandelion tea regularly can aid in a quicker recovery and reduce the risk of complications associated with strep throat.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Dandelion's antibacterial compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxacoside, help combat the bacterial infection caused by streptococcus pyogenes, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in dandelion tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and tonsils associated with strep throat.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress caused by inflammation and infection, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications such as kidney or heart problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with strep throat.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits, also known as seed heads, contain antioxidants that help fight off bacterial infections like strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves, wash them thoroughly with clean water.
Chop the dandelion roots into small pieces and measure out 10-15 grams of dried root material.
Combine the chopped dandelion roots with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea three times a day, one hour after meals, and store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with strep throat because its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the throat, alleviating discomfort and pain. The tea's antimicrobial compounds also work to combat the Streptococcus bacteria that cause strep throat, preventing further infection and promoting recovery. Additionally, stinging nettle's antihistamine properties help ease congestion and scratchiness, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable healing process. Overall, drinking stinging nettle teas can provide relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of strep throat.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Stinging nettle tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by strep throat, allowing the body to recover more efficiently.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-sitosterol and quercetin, which help alleviate the discomforting symptoms of strep throat, such as sore throats, swollen lymph nodes, and fever.

Antimicrobial properties: Stinging nettle tea's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria that cause strep throat (Streptococcus pyogenes), reducing the severity and duration of the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat associated with strep throat.
Buds: Nettle buds are rich in antioxidants and have antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections like strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine one tablespoon of dried nettle leaves with eight ounces of boiling filtered water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes allowing the tea to reach a comfortable temperature.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids immediately.
Drink the resulting tea three times a day as needed or as directed by a healthcare professional.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat strep throat with herbal teas is to combine a soothing tea blend of slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root to calm the throat irritation. Add a pinch of turmeric powder for its anti-inflammatory properties. Drink at least 3-4 cups a day to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Ginger tea can also be added to aid digestion and combat nausea. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as treatment, especially if experiencing severe symptoms or complications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to strep throat that are treated with herbal teas are pharyngitis, tonsillitis, and laryngitis. Herbal teas such as slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation. Yerba mate tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, while eucalyptus and peppermint teas provide decongestant relief for congested sinuses and a scratchy throat. These herbal teas offer a natural and comforting way to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
